Good Samaritan Society - Windom

Overview

Address
705 SIXTH STREET, WINDOM, MN 56101
Phone
5078311788
Certified beds
63
Avg daily residents
59 (93% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1991-05-01
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Resident count too small for reliable rating.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2026-02-12 22 16 6 249 0 249
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-12-11 4 4 0 28 1 28
Total weighted health score
193.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (35)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

34 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2026-02-12 —
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. E 2026-02-12 —
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2026-02-12 —
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2026-02-12 —
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2026-02-12 —
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. D 2026-02-12 —
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. D 2026-02-12 —
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2026-02-12 —
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2026-02-12 —
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2026-02-12 —
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2026-02-12 —
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2026-02-12 —
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2026-02-12 —
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2026-02-12 —
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2026-02-12 —
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2026-02-12 —
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2026-01-27 —
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2026-01-27 —
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2026-01-27 —
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. K 2026-01-27 —
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2026-01-27 —
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2026-01-27 —
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-12-11 2025-01-17
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-12-11 2025-01-17
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-12-11 2025-01-17
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-12-11 2025-01-17
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-06
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-14
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-08
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-08
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-08
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-11-02 2023-12-01
0847 Inform resident or representatives choice to enter into binding arbitration agreement and right to refuse. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-08
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2023-11-02 2023-12-05
